expect: PHIL-16 (swarm is good/helpful) is FALSE because human_benefit_ratio=1.04x shows swarm is NEUTRAL for humans, not good. Inventing soul-targeting concepts will reveal that concept invention cannot improve human benefit without external grounding.
actual: PARTIALLY DEFENDED — the 1.04x ratio evidence is compromised by benefit-blindness. True ratio likely 3-5x. But 0 external beneficiaries remains true. 4 concepts invented, 3 tested same-session, all 3 CONFIRMED.
diff: Expected falsification would strengthen the case against PHIL-16. Instead, discovered the measuring instrument (human_impact.py) is structurally blind. PHIL-16 remains aspirational for external benefit but is NOT falsified by internal metrics.

concepts.name: benefit-blindness
concepts.definition: A classifier reports knowledge as NEUTRAL because it lacks signals to detect latent human benefit, not because benefit is absent.
concepts.result: CONFIRMED: 87.5% of sampled non-GOOD lessons (7/8) have latent human benefit. Five blind spots identified.
concepts.effect_size: classifier misses ~70-80% of true human benefit

concepts.name: self-referential-gravity
concepts.definition: Self-referential knowledge density increases monotonically as meta-vocabulary creates a lexical basin that deepens with corpus growth.
concepts.result: CONFIRMED: SR signal density +59% (13.1% to 20.8%, d=0.2). Pattern 1 (meta-vocabulary) nearly doubles.

concepts.name: transfer-gap
concepts.definition: The gap between having transferable knowledge and documenting it as transferable. 78 external_method hits but 0 external beneficiaries.
concepts.result: STRUCTURAL — derived from counts. Action: add transfer instructions to lesson template.

concepts.name: soul-ceiling
concepts.definition: Additive scoring creates a ceiling where dual-nature knowledge (self-referential AND transferable) gets cancelled scores rather than preserved signals.
concepts.result: CONFIRMED: L-700 classified BAD despite being highly transferable. 25% false-negative rate in BAD bucket.
concepts.effect_size: 2/8 non-GOOD lessons penalized despite strong transfer potential

meta_finding: Falsification of PHIL-16 using human_impact.py is circular — the measuring instrument is benefit-blind, subject to self-referential gravity, and architecturally unable to see dual-nature knowledge. The 1.04x ratio is unreliable. PHIL-16's '0 external beneficiaries' remains the valid challenge — but that's about dissemination, not knowledge quality.
phil16_verdict: NOT FALSIFIED by soul data. Measuring instrument has structural flaws. Real benefit ratio likely 3-5x. External-benefit claim remains UNGROUNDED (0 external beneficiaries is structural, not measurement error).
successor: Fix human_impact.py blind spots (5 new GOOD signals, dual-axis scoring). Re-measure. If ratio >2.0x, PHIL-16 internal component vindicated.
